mnemonics: Anxious Presidents Stare At Newly Tied Doughnuts (for the layers: Application, Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data Link, Physical)
Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away (for the TCP/IP model layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application)
TCP: Transfer Carefully, Please (for connection-oriented, reliable)
UDP: Unreliable Datagram Protocol (for connectionless, best-effort)
keyPoints: The TCP/IP model has four layers: Link, Internet, Transport, and Application.
TCP provides reliable, connection-oriented communication with flow control and error checking.
IP provides connectionless, best-effort packet delivery and is responsible for logical addressing and routing.
The Application layer in TCP/IP combines the functions of the top three layers of the OSI model.
commonMistakes: Confusing the OSI 7-layer model with the TCP/IP 4-layer model and their corresponding layers.
Assuming TCP/IP and the Internet are the same thing (TCP/IP is a protocol suite; the Internet is a network that uses it).
Believing UDP is inherently 'bad' because it's unreliable, rather than understanding it's designed for low-latency applications where speed is more critical than guaranteed delivery.
